food_subgroup: Battered and fried seafood with seasoned fries combo

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined prawn pops and seasoned fries combo. FOODON_03315091 (shrimp food product) is used as the closest parent for the prawn component. The fries component is not separately anchored in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM (Shrimps and prawns) is the closest parent for the prawn component. No composite combo code exists in FoodEx2 for a prawn pops and fries meal combination.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227144002 (Prawn) is used as the closest parent substance anchor. No SNOMED CT concept exists for a prepared prawn pops and fries combo product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard combo serving consists of approximately 280 g total, comprising a portion of battered and fried prawn pops together with a regular serving of seasoned fries, as served at Potato Corner Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Fried shrimp (battered) and French fries composite estimate — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Crustaceans, shrimp, mixed species, cooked, breaded and fried (NDB 15152) and Potatoes, french fried, all types, salt added in processing, frozen, oven-heated (NDB 11401)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for Mega Prawn Pops Combo. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for breaded and fried shrimp (approximately 40% of serving weight, ~112 g) and seasoned French fries (approximately 60% of serving weight, ~168 g). Per-100g values are derived from this composite blend. Seasoning powder contribution to sodium is estimated from typical flavoured fries seasoning data. Values represent a reasonable central estimate; actual restaurant product may vary.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of French fries and battered fried foods — analogous estimate based on: French fries GI 63 (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, 2008, International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mega Prawn Pops Combo. The combo is dominated by seasoned French fries (~60% by weight). GI is estimated at 63 based on the published GI of French fries (GI 63, medium) from Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care). The battered prawn component has a lower GI contribution due to protein and fat content, but the fries dominate the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (63 × 31.8 g carb per 100g × 280 g serving / 100) / 100 = approximately 56.6 g GL units. This is a high glycemic load per serving due to large portion size.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from seasoning powder and frying process
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g, a full combo serving (~280 g) delivers around 1176 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals; patients with hypertension should limit portion size or choose unseasoned alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from seasoned fries and refined carbohydrate coating
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combo carries a high glycemic load per serving (estimated GL ~57) driven by the large portion of seasoned fries with a medium GI of 63; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict portion size significantly, pair with non-starchy vegetables, and monitor postprandial blood glucose response carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The deep-fried preparation contributes approximately 13.5 g fat per 100 g including saturated and trans fats, combined with high sodium (~420 mg/100 g) and dietary cholesterol (~72 mg/100 g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence and limit frequency of consumption in line with heart-healthy dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mega Prawn Pops Combo is a fast-food combo meal sold at Potato Corner outlets in Singapore, consisting of battered and deep-fried prawn pops paired with a regular serving of Potato Corner's signature seasoned fries. The customer selects one flavouring from Potato Corner's range of seasoning powders — options typically include BBQ, cheese, sour cream, and chilli — which are applied to both the fries and optionally the prawn pops. The prawn pops are bite-sized pieces of prawn encased in a light crispy batter, deep-fried to a golden colour, delivering a juicy, tender prawn interior with a crunchy exterior. The fries are thin-cut, crispy, and heavily coated in the chosen seasoning powder, which is the signature characteristic of Potato Corner's product. Nutritionally, the combo is energy-dense at approximately 278 kcal per 100 g, with a significant carbohydrate load from the fries (approximately 31.8 g/100 g) and moderate protein from the prawn component (approximately 8.2 g/100 g). The deep-frying process contributes 13.5 g fat per 100 g. Sodium content is elevated at approximately 420 mg per 100 g due to the seasoning powder, making this a high-sodium meal. The glycemic index is estimated at 63 (medium), but the glycemic load per full serving is high (~57) due to the large portion of fries. Clinically, this combo is of concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, high glycemic load, and fat content. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary component.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists for Mega Prawn Pops Combo from Potato Corner Singapore in any approved primary source (HPB Foodo, USDA, ICMR-NIN). Values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for breaded fried shrimp and seasoned French fries. The composite ratio (40% prawn pops, 60% fries by weight) is a reasonable approximation based on typical fast-food combo proportions. GI is estimated by analogy from published French fries GI data. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for breaded fried shrimp (NDB 15152) and seasoned French fries (NDB 11401), weighted approximately 40:60 by serving mass. Actual nutrient content will vary depending on Potato Corner's specific recipe, frying oil, and seasoning formulation. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 3. GI is estimated at 63 based on the published GI of French fries from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement exists for this product. 4. Sodium content may be higher in practice depending on the seasoning flavour selected; cheese and BBQ flavours typically carry higher sodium loads than plain. 5. Halal status is assumed based on Potato Corner Singapore's general halal certification; consumers should verify current certification status directly with the restaurant. 6. The combo is sold at Potato Corner - Funan, Singapore, as identified in the field survey source record. 7. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate.
